formulas.label: Abs
formulas.title:
Returns the absolute value (i.e. the modulus) of a supplied number. 
Example: ABS(-5) returns 5.

formulas.label: Acos
formulas.title:
Returns the Arccosine of a number 
 Example: ACOS(0.5) returns 60 degrees (approximately 1.047 radians).

formulas.label: And
formulas.title:
Tests a number of user-defined conditions and returns TRUE if ALL of the conditions evaluate to TRUE, or FALSE otherwise. 
 Example: AND(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE) returns FALSE.

formulas.label: Asin
formulas.title:
Returns the Arcsine of a number. 
 Example:  ASIN(0.5) returns 0.524 (approximately).

formulas.label: Atan
formulas.title:
Returns the Arctangent of a given number. 
 Example:  ATAN(1) returns 0.785 (approximately).

formulas.label: Atan2
formulas.title:
Returns the Arctangent of a given pair of x and y coordinates. 
 Example:  ATAN2(4, 2) returns 1.107 (approximately).

formulas.label: Average
formulas.title:
Returns the Average of a list of supplied numbers. 
 Example:  AVERAGE(1, 3, 5) returns 3.

formulas.label: Bitand
formulas.title:
Returns a Bitwise 'And' of two numbers . 
 Example: BITAND(5-101, 3-011) returns 1-001.

formulas.label: Bitlshift
formulas.title:
Returns a number shifted left by a specified number of bits. 
 Example:  BITLSHIFT(5-00000101, 2-00000010) returns 20-00010100.

formulas.label: Bitor
formulas.title:
Returns a Bitwise 'Or' of two numbers. 
 Example:  BITOR(5-0101, 2-0010) returns 7-0111.

formulas.label: Bitrshift
formulas.title:
Returns a number shifted right by a specified number of bits. 
 Example:  BITRSHIFT(5-00000101, 2-00000010) returns 1-00000001.

formulas.label: Bitxor
formulas.title:
Returns a Bitwise 'Exclusive Or' of two numbers. 
 Example: BITXOR(5-0101, 2-0010) returns 7-0111

formulas.label: Cast
formulas.title:
The CAST function is used to explicitly convert data types. It allows you to convert an expression from one data type to another.   
 Example: CAST(column_name,INT)

formulas.label: Ceiling
formulas.title:
Rounds a number away from zero (i.e. rounds a positive number up and a negative number down), to a multiple of significance. 
 Example:  CEILING(2.3) returns 3.

formulas.label: Choose
formulas.title:
Returns one of a list of values, depending on the value of a supplied index number. 
 Example:  CHOOSE(3, 'Apple', 'Banana', 'Cherry') returns 'Cherry'.

formulas.label: Clean
formulas.title:
Removes all non-printable characters from a supplied text string. 
 Example:  CLEAN('Hello!^@#$') returns 'Hello!'.

formulas.label: Coalesce
formulas.title:
The COALESCE function returns the first non-null expression in a list of . 
 Example: COALESCE(column1,'DefaultValue')

formulas.label: Code
formulas.title:
Returns the numeric code for the first character of a supplied string. 
 Example:  CODE('A') returns 65.

formulas.label: Concat
formulas.title:
Joins together two or more text strings.  
 Example:  CONCAT('Hello', ' ', 'World!') returns 'Hello World!'.

formulas.label: Concatenate
formulas.title:
Joins together two or more text strings. 
 Example:  CONCATENATE('Hello', ' ', 'World!') returns 'Hello World!'.

formulas.label: Cos
formulas.title:
Returns the Cosine of a given angle. 
 Example: COS(1) returns 0.5403 (approximately).

formulas.label: Cot
formulas.title:
Returns the cotangent of an angle. 
 Example:  COT(1) returns 0.6421 (approximately).

formulas.label: Date
formulas.title:
Returns a date, from a user-supplied year, month and day. 
 Example:  DATE(2023, 6, 29) returns June 29, 2023.

formulas.label: Day
formulas.title:
Returns the day (of the month) from a user-supplied date. 
 Example:  DAY(DATE(2023, 6, 29)) returns 29.

formulas.label: Days
formulas.title:
Returns the number of days between 2 dates. 
 Example:  DAYS(DATE(2023, 6, 29), DATE(2023, 7, 5)) returns 6.

formulas.label: Degrees
formulas.title:
Converts Radians to Degrees. 
 Example:  DEGREES(1.5708) returns 90.

formulas.label: Delta
formulas.title:
Tests whether two supplied numbers are equal. 
 Example:  DELTA(5, 5) returns 1.

formulas.label: Duplicate
formulas.title:
Returns shallow copy of existing column 
 Example:  DUPLICATE(column_name) returns column_name.

formulas.label: Edate
formulas.title:
Returns a date that is the specified number of months before or after an initial supplied start date. 
 Example:  EDATE(DATE(2023, 6, 29), 3) returns September 29, 2023.

formulas.label: Even
formulas.title:
Rounds a number away from zero (i.e. rounds a positive number up and a negative number down), to the next even number. 
 Example:  EVEN(5.5) returns 6.

formulas.label: Exact
formulas.title:
Tests if two supplied text strings are exactly the same and if so, returns TRUE; Otherwise, returns FALSE. (case-sensitive). 
 Example:  EXACT('apple', 'Apple') returns FALSE.

formulas.label: Exp
formulas.title:
Returns e raised to a given power. 
 Example:  EXP(1) returns 2.7183 (approximately).

formulas.label: False
formulas.title:
Simply returns the logical value FALSE. 
 Example:  FALSE() returns the logical value FALSE.

formulas.label: Find
formulas.title:
Returns the position of a supplied character or text string from within a supplied text string (case-sensitive). 
 Example:  FIND('world', 'Hello, world!') returns 8.

formulas.label: Fixed
formulas.title:
Rounds a supplied number to a specified number of decimal places, and then converts this into text. 
 Example:  FIXED(3.14159, 2) returns '3.14'.

formulas.label: Floor
formulas.title:
Rounds a number towards zero, (i.e. rounds a positive number down and a negative number up), to a multiple of significance. 
 Example:  FLOOR(4.7, 1) returns 4.

formulas.label: Gestep
formulas.title: Tests whether a number is greater than a supplied threshold value

formulas.label: Hour
formulas.title:
Returns the hour part of a user-supplied time. 
 Example:  HOUR(TIME(9, 30, 0)) returns 9.

formulas.label: If
formulas.title:
Tests a user-defined condition and returns one result if the condition is TRUE, and another result if the condition is FALSE. 
 Example:  IF(column_name > 10, 'Large', 'Small') returns 'Large' if the value in column_name is greater than 10, otherwise 'Small'.

formulas.label: Ifna
formulas.title:
Tests if an expression returns the #N/A error and if so, returns an alternative specified value; Otherwise the function returns the value of the supplied expression. 
 Example:  IFNA(VLOOKUP(A1, B1:C10, 2, FALSE), 'Not found') returns 'Not found' if the VLOOKUP result is #N/A.

formulas.label: Ifs
formulas.title:
Tests a number of supplied conditions and returns a result corresponding to the first condition that evaluates to TRUE. 
 Example:  IFS(A1 > 10, 'Large', A1 > 5, 'Medium', A1 > 0, 'Small') returns 'Large' if A1 is greater than 10, 'Medium' if A1 is greater than 5, and 'Small' if A1 is greater than 0.

formulas.label: Int
formulas.title:
Rounds a number down to the next integer. 
 Example:  INT(3.8) returns 3.

formulas.label: Isblank
formulas.title:
Tests if a given column_name is blank (empty), and if so, returns TRUE; Otherwise, returns FALSE. 
 Example:  ISBLANK(column_name) returns TRUE if column_name is empty.

formulas.label: Iseven
formulas.title:
Tests if a supplied number (or expression) is an even number, and if so, returns TRUE; Otherwise, returns FALSE. 
 Example:  ISEVEN(6) returns TRUE.

formulas.label: Isin
formulas.title:
Returns True/False 
 Example:  ISIN(column_name,1,2,3) returns True 
if  column_name is among given rest of value(1,2,3), 
False otherwise.

formulas.label: Isna
formulas.title:
Tests if an initial supplied value (or expression) or column returns the #N/A error and if so, returns TRUE; Otherwise returns FALSE. 
 Example:  ISNA(column_name) returns TRUE if column contains #N/A.

formulas.label: Isnumber
formulas.title:
Tests if a supplied value is a number, and if so, returns TRUE; Otherwise, returns FALSE. 
 Example:  ISNUMBER(column_name) returns TRUE if column column_name contains a number.

formulas.label: Isodd
formulas.title:
Tests if a supplied number (or expression) is an odd number, and if so, returns TRUE; Otherwise, returns FALSE. 
 Example:  ISODD(7) returns TRUE.

formulas.label: Isoweeknum
formulas.title:
Returns the ISO week number of the year for a given date (New in Excel 2013). 
 Example:  ISOWEEKNUM(DATE(2023, 6, 29)) returns 26.

formulas.label: Istext
formulas.title:
Tests if a supplied value is text, and if so, returns TRUE; Otherwise, returns FALSE. 
 Example:  ISTEXT(A1) returns TRUE if column A1 contains text.

formulas.label: Left
formulas.title:
Returns a specified number of characters from the start of a supplied text string. 
 Example:  ISTEXT(A1) returns TRUE if column A1 contains text.

formulas.label: Len
formulas.title:
Returns the length of a supplied text string. 
 Example:  LEN('Hello') returns 5.

formulas.label: Ln
formulas.title:
Returns the natural logarithm of a given number. 
 Example:  LN(10) returns approximately 2.3026.

formulas.label: Log
formulas.title:
Returns the logarithm of a given number, to a specified base. 
 Example: LOG(100, 10) returns 2.

formulas.label: Log10
formulas.title:
Returns the base 10 logarithm of a given number. 
 Example:  LOG10(100) returns 2.

formulas.label: Lower
formulas.title:
Converts all characters in a supplied text string to lower case. 
 Example:  LOWER('HELLO') returns 'hello'.

formulas.label: Max
formulas.title:
Returns the largest value from a list of supplied numbers or columns. 
 Example:  MAX(column_1, column_2, 2,3,5) returns the maximum value from given inputs.

formulas.label: Mid
formulas.title:
Returns a specified number of characters from the middle of a supplied text string. 
 Example:  MID('Hello, world!', 8, 5) returns 'world'.

formulas.label: Min
formulas.title:
Returns the smallest value from a list of supplied numbers. 
 Example:  MIN(column_1,column_2, 1,2,5) returns the minimum value among all values

formulas.label: Minute
formulas.title:
Returns the minute part of a user-supplied time. 
 Example: MINUTE(TIME(9, 30, 0)) returns 30.

formulas.label: Mod
formulas.title:
Returns the remainder from a division between two supplied numbers. 
 Example:  MOD(10, 3) returns 1.

formulas.label: Month
formulas.title:
Returns the month from a user-supplied date. 
 Example:  MONTH(DATE(2023, 6, 29)) returns 6.

formulas.label: N
formulas.title:
Converts a non-number value to a number, a date to a serial number, the logical value TRUE to 1 and all other values to 0. 
 Example:  N('10') returns 10.

formulas.label: Not
formulas.title:
Returns a logical value that is the opposite of a user supplied logical value or expression. 
 Example:  NOT(TRUE) returns FALSE.

formulas.label: Notin
formulas.title:
Returns True/False 
 Example:  NOTIN(column_name,1,2,3) returns True 
if column_name is not among given rest of value(1,2,3), 
False otherwise.

formulas.label: Now
formulas.title:
Returns the current date & time. 
 Example:  NOW() returns the current date and time.

formulas.label: Numbervalue
formulas.title:
Converts text to a number, in a locale-independent way. 
 Example:  NUMBERVALUE('123.45', '.', ',') returns 123.45.

formulas.label: Odd
formulas.title:
Rounds a number away from zero (i.e. rounds a positive number up and a negative number down), to the next odd number. 
 Example:  ODD(7.5) returns 9.

formulas.label: Or
formulas.title:
Tests a number of user-defined conditions and returns TRUE if ANY of the conditions evaluate to TRUE, or FALSE otherwise. 
 Example:  OR(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE) returns TRUE.

formulas.label: Pi
formulas.title:
Returns the constant value of pi. 
 Example:  PI() returns the value of pi (approximately 3.14159265358979).

formulas.label: Power
formulas.title:
Returns the result of a given number raised to a supplied power. 
 Example:  POWER(2, 3) returns 8.

formulas.label: Product
formulas.title:
Returns the product of a supplied list of numbers. 
 Example:  PRODUCT(2, 3, 4) returns 24.

formulas.label: Proper
formulas.title:
Converts all characters in a supplied text string to proper case (i.e. letters that do not follow another letter are upper case and all other characters are lower case). 
 Example:  PROPER('hello world') returns 'Hello World'.

formulas.label: Quotient
formulas.title:
Returns the integer portion of a division between two supplied numbers. 
 Example:  QUOTIENT(10, 3) returns 3.

formulas.label: Radians
formulas.title:
Converts Degrees to Radians. 
 Example:  RADIANS(45) returns 0.785 (approximately).

formulas.label: Rept
formulas.title:
Returns a string consisting of a supplied text string, repeated a specified number of times. 
 Example:  REPT('Hello', 3) returns 'HelloHelloHello'.

formulas.label: Right
formulas.title:
Returns a specified number of characters from the end of a supplied text string. 
 Example:  RIGHT('Hello, world!', 6) returns 'world!''.

formulas.label: Round
formulas.title:
Rounds a number up or down, to a given number of digits. 
 Example:  ROUND(3.14159, 2) returns 3.14.

formulas.label: Rounddown
formulas.title:
Rounds a number towards zero, (i.e. rounds a positive number down and a negative number up), to a given number of digits. 
 Example:  ROUNDDOWN(3.14159, 2) returns 3.14.

formulas.label: Roundup
formulas.title:
Rounds a number away from zero (i.e. rounds a positive number up and a negative number down), to a given number of digits. 
 Example:  ROUNDUP(3.14159, 2) returns 3.15.

formulas.label: Search
formulas.title:
Returns the position of a supplied character or text string from within a supplied text string (non-case-sensitive). 
 Example:  SEARCH('world', 'Hello, world!') returns 8.

formulas.label: Second
formulas.title:
Returns the seconds part of a user-supplied time. 
 Example: SECOND(TIME(9, 30, 15)) returns 15.

formulas.label: Sign
formulas.title:
Returns the sign (+1, -1 or 0) of a supplied number. 
 Example:  SIGN(-5) returns -1.

formulas.label: Sin
formulas.title:
Returns the Sine of a given angle. 
 Example:  SIN(1) returns 0.8415 (approximately).

formulas.label: Sqrt
formulas.title:
Returns the positive square root of a given number. 
 Example:  SQRT(25) returns 5.

formulas.label: Sqrtpi
formulas.title:
Returns the square root of a supplied number multiplied by pi. 
 Example:  SQRTPI(10) returns 5.6419 (approximately).

formulas.label: Substitute
formulas.title:
Substitutes all occurrences of a search text string, within an original text string, with the supplied replacement text. 
 Example:  SUBSTITUTE('Hello, world!', 'world', 'universe') returns 'Hello, universe!'.

formulas.label: Sum
formulas.title:
Returns the sum of a supplied list of numbers. 
 Example:  SUM(1, 2, 3) returns 6.

formulas.label: Sumsq
formulas.title:
Returns the sum of the squares of a supplied list of numbers. 
 Example:  SUMSQ(1, 2, 3) returns 14.

formulas.label: Switch
formulas.title:
Compares a number of supplied values to a supplied test expression and returns a result corresponding to the first value that matches the test expression. 
 Example:  SWITCH(A1, 1, 'One', 2, 'Two', 3, 'Three', 'Other') returns 'One' if A1 is 1, 'Two' if A1 is 2, 'Three' if A1 is 3, and 'Other' otherwise.

formulas.label: Tan
formulas.title:
Returns the Tangent of a given angle. 
 Example:  TAN(1) returns 1.5574 (approximately).

formulas.label: Time
formulas.title:
Returns a time, from a user-supplied hour, minute and second. 
 Example:  TIME(9, 30, 0) returns the time 09:30:00.

formulas.label: Today
formulas.title:
Returns today's date. 
 Example:  TIME(9, 30, 0) returns the time 09:30:00.

formulas.label: Trim
formulas.title:
Removes duplicate spaces, and spaces at the start and end of a text string. 
 Example:  TRIM(' Hello ') returns 'Hello'.

formulas.label: True
formulas.title:
Simply returns the logical value TRUE. 
 Example:  TRUE() returns the logical value TRUE.

formulas.label: Upper
formulas.title:
Converts all characters in a supplied text string to upper case. 
 Example:  UPPER('hello') returns 'HELLO'.

formulas.label: Weekday
formulas.title:
Returns an integer representing the day of the week for a supplied date. 
 Example: WEEKDAY(DATE(2023, 6, 29)) returns 5.

formulas.label: Weeknum
formulas.title:
Returns an integer representing the week number (from 1 to 53) of the year for a supplied date. 
 Example: WEEKNUM(DATE(2023, 6, 29)) returns 26.

formulas.label: Xor
formulas.title:
Returns a logical Exclusive Or of all arguments. 
 Example:  XOR(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE) returns TRUE.

formulas.label: Year
formulas.title:
Returns the year from a user-supplied date. 
 Example:  YEAR(DATE(2023, 6, 29)) returns 2023.
